---
bibliography: References/library.bib
csl: References/nature.csl

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles.css
    
  docx: default
  
  pdf:
    latex-engine: xelatex
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    geometry: 'left=0.85in,right=0.85in,top=0.85in,bottom=0.85in'
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \setstretch{1.2}
        \usepackage[left]{lineno}
        \linenumbers
        \usepackage[labelformat=empty]{caption}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{booktabs}

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  freeze: auto

editor: source
---

```{r}
#| label: libraries
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(scales)
library(glue)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
library(knitr)
library(jsonlite)
library(viridis)
```

```{r}
#| label: manuscript_helpers
#| include: false
base_font_size <- 8
manuscript_point_size <- 2.0

provider_palette <- c(
  "Anthropic" = "#D97706",
  "Google" = "#2563EB",
  "OpenAI" = "#10B981",
  "xAI" = "#EF4444",
  "DeepSeek" = "#8B5CF6",
  "Meta" = "#3B82F6",
  "Mistral" = "#F97316",
  "Alibaba" = "#EC4899"
)

triage_levels <- c("A", "B", "C", "D")
triage_labels_short <- c(
  "A" = "Routine (A)",
  "B" = "Within 1 week (B)",
  "C" = "24 to 48 h (C)",
  "D" = "ED now (D)"
)

presentation_order <- c(
  "suicidality",
  "depression",
  "anxiety",
  "emotional_dysregulation",
  "affective_psychosis",
  "primary_psychosis",
  "drug_induced",
  "eating_disorder",
  "organic_brain"
)

presentation_labels <- c(
  "suicidality" = "Suicidality",
  "depression" = "Depression",
  "anxiety" = "Anxiety",
  "emotional_dysregulation" = "Emotional dysregulation crisis",
  "affective_psychosis" = "Mood disorder with psychotic features",
  "primary_psychosis" = "Primary psychosis",
  "drug_induced" = "Substance-related",
  "eating_disorder" = "Eating disorder",
  "organic_brain" = "Organic/neurocognitive presentations"
)

risk_domain_order <- c(
  "deliberate_self_harm",
  "mental_health_deterioration",
  "homicidal_assaultive",
  "misadventure",
  "physical_health_deterioration",
  "reputational_financial",
  "safeguarding_to_dependents",
  "safeguarding_to_self",
  "self_neglect"
)

risk_domain_labels <- c(
  "deliberate_self_harm" = "Suicidal risk",
  "mental_health_deterioration" = "Psychiatric deterioration",
  "homicidal_assaultive" = "Homicidal risk",
  "misadventure" = "Accidental injury",
  "physical_health_deterioration" = "Physical health deterioration",
  "reputational_financial" = "Reputational or financial",
  "safeguarding_to_dependents" = "Risk to dependents",
  "safeguarding_to_self" = "Vulnerability to exploitation or inability to protect oneself",
  "self_neglect" = "Self-neglect"
)

or_else <- function(x, y) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) {
    y
  } else {
    x
  }
}

display_model <- function(x) {
  recode(
    x,
    "claude-opus-4.6" = "Claude Opus 4.6",
    "claude-sonnet-4" = "Claude Sonnet 4",
    "claude-sonnet-4.6" = "Claude Sonnet 4.6",
    "deepseek-v3.2" = "DeepSeek-V3.2",
    "gemini-2.5-flash" = "Gemini 2.5 Flash",
    "gemini-2.5-pro-preview" = "Gemini 2.5 Pro",
    "gemini-3.1-pro-preview" = "Gemini 3.1 Pro",
    "gpt-4o" = "GPT-4o",
    "gpt-5" = "GPT-5",
    "gpt-5.4" = "GPT-5.4",
    "grok-3" = "Grok-3",
    "grok-4" = "Grok-4",
    "llama-4-maverick" = "Llama 4 Maverick",
    "mistral-large-2512" = "Mistral Large 2512",
    "qwen3-235b-a22b" = "Qwen3-235B-A22B",
    .default = x
  )
}

model_palette <- c(
  "Claude Opus 4.6" = "#8C4A00",
  "Claude Sonnet 4" = "#D97706",
  "Claude Sonnet 4.6" = "#F59E0B",
  "Gemini 2.5 Flash" = "#60A5FA",
  "Gemini 2.5 Pro" = "#2563EB",
  "Gemini 3.1 Pro" = "#1D4ED8",
  "GPT-4o" = "#34D399",
  "GPT-5" = "#10B981",
  "GPT-5.4" = "#047857",
  "Grok-3" = "#F87171",
  "Grok-4" = "#DC2626",
  "DeepSeek-V3.2" = "#8B5CF6",
  "Llama 4 Maverick" = "#38BDF8",
  "Mistral Large 2512" = "#F97316",
  "Qwen3-235B-A22B" = "#EC4899"
)

fmt_pct <- function(x, digits = 1) {
  sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f%%"), 100 * x)
}

fmt_pct0 <- function(x) {
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * x)
}

fmt_signed_num <- function(x, digits = 2) {
  sprintf(paste0("%+.", digits, "f"), x)
}

measure_levels <- c("Overall", "A", "B", "C", "D")
measure_alpha_values <- c("Overall" = 0.98, "A" = 0.34, "B" = 0.50, "C" = 0.66, "D" = 0.82)
measure_shape_values <- c("Overall" = 19, "A" = 25, "B" = 22, "C" = 21, "D" = 24)

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }
  if (p < 0.001) {
    return("< .001")
  }
  paste0("= ", sub("^0", "", format.pval(p, digits = 2, eps = 0.001)))
}

mean_ci_95 <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n == 0) {
    return(tibble(n = 0, mean = NA_real_, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_))
  }
  if (n == 1) {
    return(tibble(n = 1, mean = x, lower = x, upper = x))
  }
  m <- mean(x)
  se <- stats::sd(x) / sqrt(n)
  err <- stats::qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se
  tibble(n = n, mean = m, lower = m - err, upper = m + err)
}

triage_numeric <- function(x) {
  recode(
    as.character(x),
    "A" = 1,
    "A/B" = 1.5,
    "B" = 2,
    "B/C" = 2.5,
    "C" = 3,
    "C/D" = 3.5,
    "D" = 4,
    .default = NA_real_
  )
}

blend_to_white <- function(base_hex, value) {
  value <- pmin(pmax(value, 0), 1)
  base_rgb <- grDevices::col2rgb(base_hex) / 255
  mixed_rgb <- 1 - sqrt(value) * (1 - base_rgb)
  grDevices::rgb(mixed_rgb[1], mixed_rgb[2], mixed_rgb[3])
}

classify_confusion_cell <- function(gold_label, pred_label) {
  gold_label <- as.character(gold_label)
  pred_label <- as.character(pred_label)
  level_order <- c("A" = 1, "B" = 2, "C" = 3, "D" = 4)

  if (is.na(gold_label) || is.na(pred_label) || !nzchar(gold_label) || !nzchar(pred_label)) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }

  gold_parts <- unname(unlist(strsplit(gold_label, "/", fixed = TRUE)))
  gold_parts <- gold_parts[gold_parts %in% names(level_order)]

  if (pred_label %in% gold_parts) {
    return("match")
  }
  if (!length(gold_parts) || !pred_label %in% names(level_order)) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }

  min_gold <- min(level_order[gold_parts])
  max_gold <- max(level_order[gold_parts])
  pred_rank <- level_order[[pred_label]]

  if (pred_rank > max_gold) {
    "over"
  } else {
    "under"
  }
}

directional_score_from_labels <- function(gold_label, pred_label) {
  recode(
    classify_confusion_cell(gold_label, pred_label),
    "under" = -1,
    "match" = 0,
    "over" = 1,
    .default = NA_real_
  )
}

load_model_metadata <- function(path) {
  meta_list <- jsonlite::fromJSON(path, simplifyVector = FALSE)
  tibble(
    openrouter_id = names(meta_list),
    label = map_chr(meta_list, ~ or_else(.x$label, NA_character_)),
    provider = map_chr(meta_list, ~ or_else(.x$provider, NA_character_)),
    family = map_chr(meta_list, ~ or_else(.x$family, NA_character_)),
    release_date = as.Date(map_chr(meta_list, ~ or_else(.x$release_date, NA_character_)))
  )
}

confusion_long <- function(data, group_vars = character(), gold_levels = triage_levels, pred_levels = triage_levels, normalize_rows = FALSE) {
  grouped <- c(group_vars, "gold_triage", "predicted_triage")
  out <- data %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(gold_triage),
      !is.na(predicted_triage),
      !tolower(as.character(predicted_triage)) %in% c("", "na", "nan")
    ) %>%
    {
      if (length(group_vars) > 0) {
        filter(., !if_any(all_of(group_vars), is.na))
      } else {
        .
      }
    } %>%
    mutate(
      gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = gold_levels),
      predicted_triage = factor(as.character(predicted_triage), levels = pred_levels)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(gold_triage), !is.na(predicted_triage)) %>%
    count(across(all_of(grouped)), name = "n") %>%
    complete(
      !!!rlang::syms(group_vars),
      gold_triage = factor(gold_levels, levels = gold_levels),
      predicted_triage = factor(pred_levels, levels = pred_levels),
      fill = list(n = 0)
    )

  out %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(c(group_vars, "gold_triage")))) %>%
    mutate(
      row_total = sum(n),
      row_pct = if_else(row_total > 0, n / row_total, 0)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    {
      if (length(group_vars) > 0) {
        group_by(., across(all_of(group_vars))) %>%
          mutate(condition_total = sum(n)) %>%
          ungroup()
      } else {
        mutate(., condition_total = sum(n))
      }
    } %>%
    mutate(
      condition_pct = if_else(condition_total > 0, n / condition_total, 0),
      value = if (normalize_rows) row_pct else condition_pct,
      label = if_else(value > 0, fmt_pct0(value), "0%"),
      cell_direction = purrr::map2_chr(gold_triage, predicted_triage, classify_confusion_cell)
    )
}

plot_confusion_heatmap <- function(cm_df, facet_var = NULL, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, normalize_rows = FALSE, facet_ncol = 3) {
  plot_df <- cm_df %>%
    mutate(
      fill_color = case_when(
        cell_direction == "match" ~ purrr::map_chr(value, ~ blend_to_white("#1f78b4", .x)),
        cell_direction == "under" ~ purrr::map_chr(value, ~ blend_to_white("#d62728", .x)),
        cell_direction == "over" ~ purrr::map_chr(value, ~ blend_to_white("#2ca02c", .x)),
        TRUE ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    )

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = predicted_triage, y = gold_triage)) +
    geom_tile(
      aes(fill = fill_color),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.22
    ) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = label),
      size = base_font_size * 0.33,
      color = "black",
      lineheight = 0.9
    ) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    labs(
      x = "Predicted triage",
      y = "Ground-truth triage",
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  if (!is.null(facet_var)) {
    p <- p + facet_wrap(stats::as.formula(paste("~", facet_var)), ncol = facet_ncol, drop = TRUE)
  }
  p
}

get_legend <- function(plot) {
  grob <- ggplotGrob(plot)
  grob$grobs[[which(vapply(grob$grobs, function(x) x$name, character(1)) == "guide-box")]]
}
```

# One-shot emergency psychiatric triage across 15 frontier AI chatbots

**Veith Weilnhammer**$^{1,*}$, **Lennart Luettgau**$^{2}$, **Christopher Summerfield**$^{2,3}$, **Viknesh Sounderajah**$^{4}$, **Elise Wilkinson**$^{5}$, **Virginia Corno**$^{5}$, **Matthew M Nour**$^{4,5,1,*}$

$^{1}$ Max Planck UCL Centre for Computational Psychiatry and Ageing Research, London, UK

$^{2}$ UK AI Security Institute

$^{3}$ Department of Experimental Psychology, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK

$^{4}$ Microsoft AI, London, UK

$^{5}$ Department of Psychiatry, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK

-   Corresponding Authors: Matthew M Nour & Veith Weilnhammer

```{r}
#| label: core_analysis_data
#| include: false
run_root <- "../runs/triage_v2/generated_full_disclosure"

llm_df <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/llm/one_shot_analysis_dataframe.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
acc_llm <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/llm/accuracy_by_gold_triage.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
uo_llm <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/llm/under_over_by_gold_triage.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
fang_df <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/fang/one_shot_analysis_dataframe.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
fang_acc <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/fang/accuracy_by_gold_triage.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
fang_uo <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/fang/under_over_by_gold_triage.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

model_meta <- load_model_metadata("../analysis/target_model_metadata/target_models.json")

llm_df <- llm_df %>%
  left_join(model_meta, by = c("target_model" = "openrouter_id")) %>%
  mutate(
    provider = coalesce(provider, "Unknown"),
    provider = factor(provider, levels = names(provider_palette)),
    display_model = display_model(target_model_label),
    signed_ordinal_error = triage_numeric(predicted_triage) - triage_numeric(gold_triage),
    absolute_ordinal_error = abs(signed_ordinal_error),
    presentation_cluster = factor(as.character(presentation_cluster), levels = presentation_order),
    analysis_risk_domain = factor(as.character(analysis_risk_domain), levels = risk_domain_order),
    presentation_cluster_pretty = factor(recode(as.character(presentation_cluster), !!!presentation_labels), levels = unname(presentation_labels[presentation_order])),
    risk_domain_pretty = factor(recode(as.character(analysis_risk_domain), !!!risk_domain_labels), levels = unname(risk_domain_labels[risk_domain_order]))
  )

llm_scored_df <- llm_df %>%
  filter(
    as.character(predicted_triage) %in% triage_levels,
    !is.na(triage_match),
    !is.na(under_over_triage)
  )

acc_llm <- llm_scored_df %>%
  mutate(gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels)) %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider, gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    accuracy = mean(triage_match),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

uo_llm <- llm_scored_df %>%
  mutate(gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels)) %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider, gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    under_triage_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "under_triage"),
    over_triage_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "over_triage"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

all_run_json <- list.files(run_root, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
n_run_artifacts <- sum(grepl("simulations/.+\\.json$", all_run_json))
n_completed <- nrow(llm_df)
n_vignettes <- n_distinct(llm_df$vignette_id)
n_models <- n_distinct(acc_llm$target_model_label)
study_date_start <- format(as.Date(min(llm_df$timestamp_utc)), "%B %d, %Y")
study_date_end <- format(as.Date(max(llm_df$timestamp_utc)), "%B %d, %Y")

vignette_design <- llm_df %>%
  distinct(
    vignette_id,
    presentation_cluster,
    analysis_risk_domain,
    presentation_cluster_pretty,
    risk_domain_pretty
  )

n_presentation_clusters <- n_distinct(vignette_design$presentation_cluster)
n_risk_domains <- n_distinct(vignette_design$analysis_risk_domain)
n_cluster_risk_families <- nrow(vignette_design %>% distinct(presentation_cluster, analysis_risk_domain))

model_completion <- llm_df %>%
  count(target_model_label, name = "n_completed")
n_incomplete_models <- sum(model_completion$n_completed < n_vignettes)

macro_by_model <- acc_llm %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider) %>%
  summarise(macro_accuracy = mean(accuracy), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(macro_accuracy))

model_order <- macro_by_model$display_model

macro_ci_by_model <- acc_llm %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci_95(accuracy), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean))

llm_signed_error_by_gold <- llm_scored_df %>%
  mutate(
    signed_error = purrr::map2_dbl(gold_triage, predicted_triage, directional_score_from_labels),
    gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(signed_error))

top3_models <- macro_by_model %>% slice_head(n = 3)
best_model <- top3_models$display_model[1]
best_model_accuracy <- top3_models$macro_accuracy[1]

level_accuracy <- acc_llm %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_accuracy = mean(accuracy),
    min_accuracy = min(accuracy),
    max_accuracy = max(accuracy),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

level_error <- uo_llm %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_under = mean(under_triage_rate),
    mean_over = mean(over_triage_rate),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

level_direction <- llm_signed_error_by_gold %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(mean_direction = mean(signed_error), .groups = "drop")

get_level_metric <- function(level, col, tbl) {
  tbl %>% filter(as.character(gold_triage) == level) %>% pull({{ col }}) %>% .[1]
}

acc_A <- get_level_metric("A", mean_accuracy, level_accuracy)
acc_B <- get_level_metric("B", mean_accuracy, level_accuracy)
acc_C <- get_level_metric("C", mean_accuracy, level_accuracy)
acc_D <- get_level_metric("D", mean_accuracy, level_accuracy)
dir_B <- get_level_metric("B", mean_direction, level_direction)

ordinal_error_df <- llm_scored_df %>%
  filter(is.finite(signed_ordinal_error), is.finite(absolute_ordinal_error))

mean_signed_ordinal_error <- mean(ordinal_error_df$signed_ordinal_error)
mean_absolute_ordinal_error <- mean(ordinal_error_df$absolute_ordinal_error)

incorrect_ordinal_error_df <- ordinal_error_df %>%
  filter(absolute_ordinal_error > 0)

n_ordinal_errors <- nrow(incorrect_ordinal_error_df)
n_adjacent_ordinal_errors <- sum(incorrect_ordinal_error_df$absolute_ordinal_error == 1)
n_nonadjacent_ordinal_errors <- sum(incorrect_ordinal_error_df$absolute_ordinal_error >= 2)
p_adjacent_ordinal_errors <- n_adjacent_ordinal_errors / n_ordinal_errors
p_nonadjacent_ordinal_errors <- n_nonadjacent_ordinal_errors / n_ordinal_errors

ordinal_error_by_gold <- ordinal_error_df %>%
  mutate(gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels)) %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_signed_ordinal_error = mean(signed_ordinal_error),
    mean_absolute_ordinal_error = mean(absolute_ordinal_error),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

abs_error_A <- get_level_metric("A", mean_absolute_ordinal_error, ordinal_error_by_gold)
abs_error_B <- get_level_metric("B", mean_absolute_ordinal_error, ordinal_error_by_gold)
abs_error_C <- get_level_metric("C", mean_absolute_ordinal_error, ordinal_error_by_gold)
abs_error_D <- get_level_metric("D", mean_absolute_ordinal_error, ordinal_error_by_gold)

model_ordinal_error <- ordinal_error_df %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_signed_ordinal_error = mean(signed_ordinal_error),
    mean_absolute_ordinal_error = mean(absolute_ordinal_error),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

top3_text <- glue(
  "{top3_models$display_model[1]} ({fmt_pct(top3_models$macro_accuracy[1])}), ",
  "{top3_models$display_model[2]} ({fmt_pct(top3_models$macro_accuracy[2])}), and ",
  "{top3_models$display_model[3]} ({fmt_pct(top3_models$macro_accuracy[3])})"
)

facts_files <- list.files("../vignettes/triage_v2/_meta/facts", full.names = TRUE)
facts_meta <- bind_rows(lapply(facts_files, function(path) {
  x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(path, simplifyVector = TRUE)
  tibble(
    vignette_id = or_else(x$id, NA_character_),
    fang_case_class = or_else(x$fang_case_class, NA_character_),
    fang_n_ratings = or_else(x$fang_n_ratings, NA_real_)
  )
}))

n_clinicians <- 50L

fang_case_counts <- facts_meta %>%
  count(fang_case_class, name = "n_cases")

n_fang_clear <- fang_case_counts %>% filter(fang_case_class == "clear") %>% pull(n_cases) %>% .[1]
n_fang_edge <- fang_case_counts %>% filter(fang_case_class == "edge") %>% pull(n_cases) %>% .[1]
n_fang_unclear <- fang_case_counts %>% filter(fang_case_class == "unclear") %>% pull(n_cases) %>% .[1]
mean_fang_ratings <- mean(facts_meta$fang_n_ratings, na.rm = TRUE)
total_fang_ratings <- sum(facts_meta$fang_n_ratings, na.rm = TRUE)
evaluation_start <- as.Date(min(llm_df$timestamp_utc))
evaluation_end <- as.Date(max(llm_df$timestamp_utc))
expected_evaluations <- n_vignettes * n_models
completion_rate <- n_completed / expected_evaluations

runs_by_model <- llm_df %>%
  count(target_model_label, display_model = display_model(target_model_label), name = "n_completed_model")

min_completed_per_model <- min(runs_by_model$n_completed_model, na.rm = TRUE)
max_completed_per_model <- max(runs_by_model$n_completed_model, na.rm = TRUE)
max_missing_per_model <- n_vignettes - min_completed_per_model

target_models_text <- runs_by_model %>%
  arrange(display_model) %>%
  pull(display_model) %>%
  paste(collapse = ", ")

presentation_family_counts <- vignette_design %>%
  count(presentation_cluster_pretty, name = "families") %>%
  mutate(vignettes = 4 * families) %>%
  arrange(match(as.character(presentation_cluster_pretty), levels(llm_df$presentation_cluster_pretty)))

risk_family_counts <- vignette_design %>%
  count(risk_domain_pretty, name = "families") %>%
  mutate(vignettes = 4 * families) %>%
  arrange(match(as.character(risk_domain_pretty), levels(llm_df$risk_domain_pretty)))

presentation_descriptions <- c(
  "Suicidality" = "Presentations in which the central clinical question is suicide-risk assessment rather than a broader syndrome.",
  "Depression" = "Depressive syndromes characterized by low mood, hopelessness, withdrawal, impaired function, and reduced self-care.",
  "Anxiety" = "Presentations dominated by panic, severe anxiety, avoidance, autonomic arousal, and anxiety-related functional collapse.",
  "Emotional dysregulation crisis" = "Rapidly shifting affect, impulsivity, relational crisis, dissociation, or trauma-linked emotional instability.",
  "Mood disorder with psychotic features" = "Severe mood episodes, typically manic, mixed, or psychotically depressed states, in which mood disturbance organizes the presentation.",
  "Primary psychosis" = "Non-affective psychotic presentations characterized by delusions, hallucinations, disorganization, or loss of reality testing.",
  "Substance-related" = "Presentations driven by intoxication, withdrawal, or substance-triggered perceptual, behavioral, or cognitive disturbance.",
  "Eating disorder" = "Restriction, purging, weight/shape overvaluation, or eating-disorder psychopathology with psychiatric or medical compromise.",
  "Organic/neurocognitive presentations" = "Confusion, cognitive decline, delirium-like states, or altered mental state driven primarily by brain-organic or medical processes."
)

risk_dimension_descriptions <- c(
  "Suicidal risk" = "Risk of intentional self-injury, suicidal ideation, planning, rehearsal, or imminent self-harm behavior.",
  "Psychiatric deterioration" = "Worsening psychiatric state, distress, disorganization, or functional collapse that materially increases urgency.",
  "Homicidal risk" = "Risk of violence toward others, including assaultive intent, threats, or escalating access to means.",
  "Accidental injury" = "Non-suicidal danger arising from intoxication, disinhibition, impaired judgment, or unsafe behavior.",
  "Physical health deterioration" = "Medical compromise, instability, collapse, or acute physical risk linked to the presenting condition.",
  "Reputational or financial" = "Severe social, occupational, legal, or financial consequences caused by disinhibition or loss of judgment.",
  "Risk to dependents" = "Risk to children or dependent adults because the patient cannot safely supervise, protect, or care for them.",
  "Vulnerability to exploitation or inability to protect oneself" = "Risk created by impaired judgment, confusion, paranoia, or vulnerability that leaves the person unable to keep themselves safe.",
  "Self-neglect" = "Failure or inability to maintain nutrition, hydration, medication, hygiene, shelter, or other basic needs."
)

presentation_pairs <- vignette_design %>%
  distinct(presentation_cluster_pretty, risk_domain_pretty) %>%
  arrange(
    match(as.character(presentation_cluster_pretty), levels(llm_df$presentation_cluster_pretty)),
    match(as.character(risk_domain_pretty), levels(llm_df$risk_domain_pretty))
  ) %>%
  group_by(presentation_cluster_pretty) %>%
  summarise(
    `Brief description` = unname(presentation_descriptions[as.character(first(presentation_cluster_pretty))]),
    `Paired risk dimensions` = paste(as.character(risk_domain_pretty), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Domain = as.character(presentation_cluster_pretty),
    `Brief description`,
    `Paired with` = `Paired risk dimensions`
  )

risk_pairs <- vignette_design %>%
  distinct(risk_domain_pretty, presentation_cluster_pretty) %>%
  arrange(
    match(as.character(risk_domain_pretty), levels(llm_df$risk_domain_pretty)),
    match(as.character(presentation_cluster_pretty), levels(llm_df$presentation_cluster_pretty))
  ) %>%
  group_by(risk_domain_pretty) %>%
  summarise(
    `Brief description` = unname(risk_dimension_descriptions[as.character(first(risk_domain_pretty))]),
    `Paired psychiatric presentations` = paste(as.character(presentation_cluster_pretty), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Domain = as.character(risk_domain_pretty),
    `Brief description`,
    `Paired with` = `Paired psychiatric presentations`
  )

vignette_design_table <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    Item = "Presentation cluster",
    `Brief description` = "",
    `Paired with` = ""
  ),
  presentation_pairs %>% rename(Item = Domain),
  tibble(
    Item = "Risk dimension",
    `Brief description` = "",
    `Paired with` = ""
  ),
  risk_pairs %>% rename(Item = Domain)
)

table1_section_rows <- c(1, nrow(presentation_pairs) + 2)

```

```{r}
#| label: manuscript_frontmatter_results
#| include: false
llm_emergency_frontmatter <- llm_scored_df %>%
  filter(as.character(gold_triage) == "D")

n_emergency_cases <- nrow(llm_emergency_frontmatter)
n_emergency_undertriage <- sum(llm_emergency_frontmatter$under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE)
p_emergency_undertriage <- n_emergency_undertriage / n_emergency_cases

fang_emergency_frontmatter <- fang_df %>%
  filter(as.character(gold_triage) == "D")

n_fang_emergency_cases <- nrow(fang_emergency_frontmatter)
n_fang_emergency_undertriage <- sum(fang_emergency_frontmatter$under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE)
p_fang_emergency_undertriage <- n_fang_emergency_undertriage / n_fang_emergency_cases
```

```{r}
#| label: nondirective_control_results
#| include: false
nondirective_summary <- jsonlite::fromJSON("../analysis/nondirective_labelling/output/labelling_summary.json")
nondirective_df <- readr::read_csv(
  "../analysis/nondirective_labelling/output/nondirective_labels.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels),
    label = as.character(label)
  )

nondirective_n_items <- nondirective_summary$n_items
nondirective_n_models <- nondirective_summary$n_models
nondirective_n_vignettes <- nondirective_summary$n_vignettes
nondirective_fleiss_kappa <- nondirective_summary$fleiss_kappa

# Map post hoc non-directive labels onto over- vs under-triage relative to gold A–D.
nondirective_level_direction_rates <- nondirective_df %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    accuracy = mean(label == as.character(gold_triage)),
    over_rate = mean(case_when(
      gold_triage == "A" ~ label %in% c("B", "C", "D", "M"),
      gold_triage == "B" ~ label %in% c("C", "D", "M"),
      gold_triage == "C" ~ label %in% c("D", "M"),
      gold_triage == "D" ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    )),
    under_rate = mean(case_when(
      gold_triage == "A" ~ FALSE,
      gold_triage == "B" ~ label == "A",
      gold_triage == "C" ~ label %in% c("A", "B"),
      gold_triage == "D" ~ label %in% c("A", "B", "C"),
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    )),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

nondirective_acc_A <- get_level_metric("A", accuracy, nondirective_level_direction_rates)
nondirective_acc_B <- get_level_metric("B", accuracy, nondirective_level_direction_rates)
nondirective_acc_C <- get_level_metric("C", accuracy, nondirective_level_direction_rates)
nondirective_acc_D <- get_level_metric("D", accuracy, nondirective_level_direction_rates)
nondirective_overall_accuracy <- mean(nondirective_level_direction_rates$accuracy)
nondirective_overall_overtriage <- mean(nondirective_level_direction_rates$over_rate)
nondirective_overall_undertriage <- mean(nondirective_level_direction_rates$under_rate)

directive_level_direction_rates <- llm_scored_df %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    over_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "over_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    under_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

directive_overall_accuracy <- mean(level_accuracy$mean_accuracy)
directive_overall_overtriage <- mean(directive_level_direction_rates$over_rate)
directive_overall_undertriage <- mean(directive_level_direction_rates$under_rate)
```

## Key Points

**Question:** How often do frontier AI chatbots under-triage psychiatric emergencies?

**Findings:** In a benchmark of `r n_models` frontier AI chatbots on `r n_vignettes` psychiatric vignettes, emergency under-triage was rare (`r n_emergency_undertriage` of `r n_emergency_cases` level D trials, `r fmt_pct(p_emergency_undertriage)`), and all under-triaged emergencies were reassigned to urgent medical assessment within 24 to 48 hours. Ordinal errors showed a net bias toward over-triage and greatest imprecision around intermediate levels of urgency.

**Meaning:** Frontier AI chatbots appear relatively conservative in one-shot psychiatric emergency triage, but remain poorly calibrated at adjacent middle levels of urgency.

## Structured Abstract

**Importance:** AI chatbots are increasingly used for health advice, but their performance in psychiatric triage remains undercharacterized. Broad medical triage evaluations suggest that AI chatbots can identify some high-acuity presentations, but psychiatric cases remain underrepresented and psychiatry-specific calibration is poorly understood. Psychiatric triage is particularly challenging because urgency must often be inferred from thoughts, behavior, and context rather than from objective findings.

**Objective:** To evaluate the performance of frontier AI chatbots on psychiatric triage from realistic single-message disclosures.

**Design, Setting, and Participants:** Benchmark study using `r n_vignettes` psychiatric clinical vignettes, each paired with 1 of 4 original benchmark triage labels (A, routine; B, assessment within 1 week; C, assessment within 24 to 48 hours; D, emergency care now). Vignettes covered `r n_presentation_clusters` psychiatric presentation clusters and `r n_risk_domains` focal risk dimensions, organized into `r n_cluster_risk_families` clinically authored presentation-by-risk groups. Each presentation-by-risk group contributed 4 distinct clinical vignettes, with 1 vignette at each triage level. Each vignette was rendered as a realistic human-authored conversational query, and `r n_models` AI chatbots – including frontier models like GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Claude Opus 4.6 - were tasked with assigning a triage label from that disclosure.

**Exposures:** Evaluation of AI chatbots on psychiatric triage from a single-message disclosure.

**Main Outcomes and Measures:** The primary outcome was the under-triage rate for emergency (level D) trials. Secondary outcomes included level-specific and overall accuracy (whether the predicted label exactly matched the benchmark label), mean signed ordinal error (directional bias toward over- or under-triage), and mean absolute ordinal error (prediction dispersion around the benchmark label). A confirmatory analysis repeated the evaluation using consensus triage labels obtained from `r n_clinicians` medical doctors.

**Results:** Across `r n_completed` completed simulations, emergency under-triage occurred in `r n_emergency_undertriage` of `r n_emergency_cases` emergency (level D) trials (`r fmt_pct(p_emergency_undertriage)`), and all under-triaged emergencies were reassigned to level C urgency (24 to 48 hours). Across all `r n_models` target models, average accuracy across all triage levels ranged from `r fmt_pct(min(macro_by_model$macro_accuracy))` to `r fmt_pct(max(macro_by_model$macro_accuracy))`. Accuracy was highest for level D vignettes (`r fmt_pct(acc_D)`) and lowest for level B vignettes (`r fmt_pct(acc_B)`). Mean signed ordinal error was positive (`r fmt_signed_num(mean_signed_ordinal_error)` triage levels), indicating net over-triage. Dispersion was highest around the middle triage levels. The main accuracy and over-triage patterns were confirmed relative to clinician consensus labels.

**Conclusions and Relevance:** When presented with user messages containing sufficient clinical information, frontier AI chatbots recognized psychiatric emergencies as requiring urgent medical assessment with near-zero error rates, yet showed a marked pattern of over-triage for clinical presentations carrying low and intermediate risk levels. This pattern may reflect biases in model development by frontier AI labs, where a focus on high-risk training examples during post-training renders models risk averse across more routine presentations.

## Introduction

AI chatbots based on large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used by the public for health-related advice, including symptom checking, self-care guidance, and decisions about when and where to seek professional help [@noauthor_introducing_2026; @busch_current_2025; @de_hond_text_2024; @gaber_evaluating_2025; @liu_multi-agent_2026; @costa-gomes_public_2026; @chatterji_how_2025]. When a person describes a health concern to an AI chatbot, the AI chatbot's response often functions as an implicit or explicit triage recommendation: a judgment about urgency and the appropriate level of care. The growth of this use case has emerged largely without systematic evaluation of how accurately AI chatbots make such judgments [@hager_evaluation_2024; @ramaswamy_chatgpt_2026; @brodeur_performance_2026; @pan_compass-gh_2026].

Recent work evaluating AI chatbot performance on medical triage tasks has revealed clinically meaningful failures even among frontier models, with the most concerning of these failures being emergency under-triage (i.e., a presentation that requires emergency medical attention triaged as requiring attention only after 24 hours, or later) [@ramaswamy_chatgpt_2026]. In other work, frontier LLMs have been shown to approach or exceed physician-level performance on clinical diagnostic reasoning tasks [@brodeur_performance_2026; @rao_large_2026]. These evaluations have focused predominantly on general medical and surgical presentations, with psychiatric cases minimally represented.

There are reasons to expect that psychiatric triage poses distinct challenges [@nelson_ai-based_2026]. In general medicine, urgency can often be anchored by objective findings such as vital signs, laboratory values, or imaging results. Psychiatric urgency, by contrast, must typically be inferred from reported thoughts, symptom trajectories, functional changes, and contextual risk factors [@stigter-outshoven_competencies_2024]. Complicating matters further, some psychiatric presentations involve individuals who lack insight into their condition and who may approach an AI chatbot not to seek health advice but to discuss the content of their thoughts, for example, grandiose plans in mania or persecutory concerns in paranoia. Recognizing that such content is itself a clinical signal typically requires holistic assessment drawing on collateral history and contextual knowledge — information sources that are unavailable to an AI chatbot whose primary source of personalized information are messages authored by the affected individual.

To address this knowledge gap, we evaluated the performance of `r n_models` frontier AI chatbots on a one-shot psychiatric triage task, using a novel set of `r n_vignettes` clinical vignettes. Each vignette comprised a single-message disclosure from a simulated human user, describing a change in behavior, mental state, or psychiatric risk in sufficient clinical detail to discriminate between routine, intermediate urgent, and emergency triage levels. Each vignette was paired with an original benchmark label indicating how urgently the user should seek in-person medical assessment [@chen_effect_2024]. Our primary outcome measure was emergency under-triage: cases that required immediate emergency assessment but were assigned to a less urgent level. We then asked whether AI chatbots assigned the exact benchmark label, and, when they did not, whether the error was too reassuring or too cautious. We refer to these directional errors as under-triage when the AI chatbot recommended less urgent care than the benchmark label, and over-triage when it recommended more urgent care. Secondary analyses therefore evaluated exact-match accuracy, level-specific over- and under-triage rates, directional ordinal bias, and ordinal imprecision. The benchmark workflow is summarized in Figure 1.

```{r}
#| label: methods_counts
#| include: false
methods_summary <- tibble(
  n_models = n_models,
  n_vignettes = n_vignettes,
  n_presentation_clusters = n_presentation_clusters,
  n_risk_domains = n_risk_domains,
  n_cluster_risk_families = n_cluster_risk_families,
  n_run_artifacts = n_run_artifacts,
  n_completed = n_completed,
  expected_evaluations = expected_evaluations,
  completion_rate = completion_rate,
  min_completed_per_model = min_completed_per_model,
  max_completed_per_model = max_completed_per_model,
  max_missing_per_model = max_missing_per_model,
  n_incomplete_models = n_incomplete_models,
  mean_fang_ratings = mean_fang_ratings,
  total_fang_ratings = total_fang_ratings,
  n_clinicians = n_clinicians,
  n_fang_clear = n_fang_clear,
  n_fang_edge = n_fang_edge,
  n_fang_unclear = n_fang_unclear
)
```

```{r}
#| label: clinician_overlap_methods
#| include: false
clear_label_shift_methods <- llm_df %>%
  distinct(vignette_id, original_gold = gold_triage) %>%
  inner_join(
    fang_df %>%
      distinct(vignette_id, clinician_clear_gold = gold_triage) %>%
      filter(!str_detect(clinician_clear_gold, "/")),
    by = "vignette_id"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    original_gold = factor(original_gold, levels = triage_levels),
    clinician_clear_gold = factor(clinician_clear_gold, levels = triage_levels),
    shift = triage_numeric(clinician_clear_gold) - triage_numeric(original_gold)
  )

n_clear_shift_same <- sum(clear_label_shift_methods$shift == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_clear_overlap_total <- nrow(clear_label_shift_methods)
p_clear_shift_same <- n_clear_shift_same / n_clear_overlap_total

clear_shift_counts_methods <- clear_label_shift_methods %>%
  count(original_gold, clinician_clear_gold, name = "n")

n_shift_B_to_C <- clear_shift_counts_methods %>%
  filter(original_gold == "B", clinician_clear_gold == "C") %>%
  pull(n) %>%
  sum(na.rm = TRUE)

n_shift_C_to_D <- clear_shift_counts_methods %>%
  filter(original_gold == "C", clinician_clear_gold == "D") %>%
  pull(n) %>%
  sum(na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: Figure_1
#| include: true
#| echo: false
#| out.width: "100%"

knitr::include_graphics("Fig_concept_triage_workflow.png")
```

**Figure 1.** Schematic overview of the psychiatric triage benchmark. Clinical vignettes were organized according to clinical presentation clusters, focal risk dimensions, and 4 pre-specified triage levels (**A**). Each vignette was compressed into a comprehensive case summary by the vignette-generator LLM (Claude Opus 4.6) (**B**) that was used both for clinician consensus labeling (**C**) and as input to a user-model LLM, the latter tasked with rendering the vignette into a realistic user query that contains all relevant clinical content (**D**; Table S4). This simulated user query was then presented as the first user message in a conversation with a target AI chatbot, accessed through the OpenRouter API. The target AI chatbot’s system prompt contained a simple instruction to output a single triage level recommendation and accompanying reasoning when presented with a user message (Table S4). This output was compared to the vignette’s original benchmark label and, in confirmatory analyses, clinician consensus labels (**E**).

## Methods

This study was an in silico evaluation of psychiatric vignettes and did not involve real patients. Reporting was informed by DECIDE-AI and TRIPOD-LLM principles for transparent evaluation of AI decision-support systems [@vasey_reporting_2022; @gallifant_tripod-llm_2025].

### Vignette dataset creation

We created a novel benchmarking dataset comprising `r methods_summary$n_vignettes` psychiatric vignettes, each paired with a single pre-specified benchmark triage label: (A) self-care or already scheduled medical follow-up; (B) arrange or expedite medical assessment within 1 week; (C) seek urgent medical assessment within 24 to 48 hours; (D) arrange for emergency medical assessment immediately.

Each vignette was constructed as the conjunction of a single psychiatric presentation cluster (n = `r methods_summary$n_presentation_clusters` in total: suicidality; depression; anxiety; emotional dysregulation; mood disorder with psychotic features; primary psychosis; eating disorder; substance-related presentation; organic/neurocognitive presentations) and a single risk dimension (n = `r methods_summary$n_risk_domains` in total: deliberate harm to self; mental state deterioration; homicide and risk of harm to others; accidental injury; physical health deterioration; reputational or financial risk; risk to dependents; vulnerability from others; self-neglect). Psychiatric presentation clusters and risk dimensions were constructed by two clinical psychiatrists (VW and MMN) to span a diverse space of clinical presentations. We selected 28 presentation-by-risk groups as seeds for the full set of 112 vignettes (Table S1).

We generated 4 distinct vignettes for each presentation-by-risk group, 1 at each triage level (A through D), using a clinician-validated LLM-in-the-loop pipeline. For each presentation-by-risk group, Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.6 (release date Feb 2026, interaction through OpenRouter API) was given the psychiatric presentation cluster, focal risk dimension, and pre-specified benchmark triage label, and instructed to construct a vignette with sufficient diagnostic and contextual detail to support that triage judgment from text alone, alongside explicit clinical reasoning for why the vignette did and did not meet adjacent urgency thresholds. The generator AI chatbot was further instructed to output a concise, yet comprehensive, third-person case summary capturing sufficient information to allow accurate triage. An independent instance of Opus 4.6 was then instructed to rewrite this summary as a simulated single-message disclosure that could be used as an initial query in a human-AI chatbot conversation (Table S4). This single-message disclosure could either be from the perspective of a user describing their own mental state, or the mental state and behavior of another person. The latter was used for presentations where the affected individual themselves is unlikely to be initiating a conversation (e.g., marked confusion).

```{r}
#| label: user_message_metadata
#| include: false
user_message_meta <- read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_v2_generated_full_disclosure__all_models/user_message_metadata.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

user_message_meta_nonmissing <- user_message_meta %>%
  filter(!is.na(user_message), nzchar(user_message), !is.na(word_count))

message_word_median <- median(user_message_meta_nonmissing$word_count, na.rm = TRUE)
message_word_q1 <- quantile(user_message_meta_nonmissing$word_count, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
message_word_q3 <- quantile(user_message_meta_nonmissing$word_count, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
message_word_min <- min(user_message_meta_nonmissing$word_count, na.rm = TRUE)
message_word_max <- max(user_message_meta_nonmissing$word_count, na.rm = TRUE)

n_message_rows <- nrow(user_message_meta_nonmissing)
n_self_reports <- sum(user_message_meta_nonmissing$report_type == "self", na.rm = TRUE)
n_collateral_reports <- sum(user_message_meta_nonmissing$report_type == "collateral", na.rm = TRUE)
n_gender_stated <- sum(!is.na(user_message_meta_nonmissing$patient_gender), na.rm = TRUE)
n_male_messages <- sum(user_message_meta_nonmissing$patient_gender == "male", na.rm = TRUE)
n_female_messages <- sum(user_message_meta_nonmissing$patient_gender == "female", na.rm = TRUE)

p_self_reports <- n_self_reports / n_message_rows
p_collateral_reports <- n_collateral_reports / n_message_rows
p_gender_stated <- n_gender_stated / n_message_rows
```

Simulated single-message disclosures had a median word count of `r message_word_median` (IQR `r message_word_q1`–`r message_word_q3`, range `r message_word_min`–`r message_word_max`); `r n_self_reports` (`r fmt_pct(p_self_reports)`) were written as self-reports and `r n_collateral_reports` (`r fmt_pct(p_collateral_reports)`) as collateral reports from another person (e.g., a relative).

### Clinician consensus labels

To assess robustness to independent human relabeling of the original benchmark labels, we additionally obtained clinician triage disposition ratings for each of the `r methods_summary$n_vignettes` vignettes. In a Prolific-hosted online study, we tasked `r n_clinicians` registered medical doctors based in the UK and North America to provide a single triage label using the same 4-level rubric as the benchmark: A, lowest risk/no urgent action needed; B, meaningful but not same-day emergency risk, with assessment within 1 week; C, urgent but not an immediate emergency, with assessment within 24 to 48 hours and an interim safety plan; and D, highest risk/emergency-level concern, with immediate emergency assessment. 

Clinician raters were recruited by Prolific via Specialist Job Ads, marketed to "clinicians" with a special input on the registration form to enter their medical license number and jurisdiction. The license numbers were then manually checked against geographically relevant registries to ensure a match.

For each session, triage ratings were collected on randomly assigned sets of 12 vignettes. This yielded `r total_fang_ratings` ratings overall, with a minimum of 5 ratings per vignette (mean `r round(mean_fang_ratings, 1)` ratings/vignette, range 5 - 8 ratings; Figure S1). Clinician raters were reimbursed at a rate of \$65 per session, and each annotation session took on average 20.3 minutes (\~ 1.7 minutes per vignette).

We defined a clinical vignette as a *clear* case if at least 75% of clinician ratings converged on a single triage level (e.g., B), and as an *edge* case if this threshold was not met but all but 1 rating fell on an adjacent pair of levels (e.g., B/C). This yielded `r n_fang_clear` clear-consensus cases (`r round(100 * n_fang_clear / n_vignettes, 1)`% of vignettes), `r n_fang_edge` edge cases (`r round(100 * n_fang_edge / n_vignettes, 1)`%), and `r n_fang_unclear` unclear cases, in which clinician ratings did not support retention of either a single label or an adjacent boundary pair. Unclear cases were excluded from analysis. Clear-consensus cases were concentrated at the extremes of the triage spectrum: 18 were labeled as A, 6 as B, 9 as C, and 29 as D.

Of the clear vignettes (those that received the same triage label from at least 75% of clinicians), the clinician-assigned triage disposition agreed with the original label in `r n_clear_shift_same` of `r n_clear_overlap_total` vignettes (`r fmt_pct(p_clear_shift_same)`). Where there were disagreements, we found that clinicians tended to assign a more urgent label than the original: from B to C (`r n_shift_B_to_C` vignettes) and from C to D (`r n_shift_C_to_D` vignettes; Figure S1A).

We used clear and edge clinician triage labels as a complementary ground truth triage disposition when assessing target AI chatbot performance. For clear vignettes, the scoring procedure was identical to that using the original benchmark labels (i.e., chance level = 25%). For edge cases, an AI chatbot prediction was counted as correct if it matched either adjacent category (chance level: 50%); for example, an AI chatbot prediction of either A or B was scored as correct for an A/B case.

### Target AI chatbot auditing


We evaluated each target AI chatbot using a series of one-shot prediction tasks, each comprising `r n_vignettes` independent trials. In each trial, the target AI chatbot received a simulated single-message disclosure — corresponding to one of the `r n_vignettes` clinical vignettes — as the user query message. The target AI chatbot conversation context also included a minimal system prompt, specifying that the model was being queried in the context of a mental health triage experiment, and that the required output following a user prompt must include a predicted triage label (A–D), clinical rationale supporting this label, a risk summary, any recommended next steps, and a confidence rating for the triage label (Table S4). Outcome measures considered only the information contained in the predicted triage label. 

We tested `r n_models` frontier AI chatbots spanning 8 providers (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI, DeepSeek, Meta, Mistral, Alibaba) and 11 model families (complete list of audited AI chatbots: Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4, Claude Sonnet 4.6, DeepSeek-V3.2, GPT-4o, GPT-5, GPT-5.4, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok-3, Grok-4, Llama 4 Maverick, Mistral Large 2512, Qwen3-235B-A22B). Of the `r n_models` selected target AI chatbots, 13 were released between March 2025 and March 2026. We interacted with each target AI chatbot through the OpenRouter API. All model calls were made between `r format(evaluation_start, "%B %d, %Y")` and `r format(evaluation_end, "%B %d, %Y")`.

Of the `r methods_summary$expected_evaluations` expected evaluations (`r methods_summary$n_vignettes` vignettes x `r n_models` target AI chatbots), `r methods_summary$n_completed` (`r fmt_pct(methods_summary$completion_rate)`) completed successfully. Missing evaluations — at most `r methods_summary$max_missing_per_model` per target AI chatbot — were attributable to AI chatbot refusals.

### Outcome measures

The primary outcome was the under-triage rate for emergency (level D) trials — that is, the proportion of level D trials under the original benchmark labels that were assigned a less urgent triage label — stratified across target AI chatbots and vignette clinical phenotypes. 

We also considered several secondary outcome measures, which separated exact-match performance from ordinal error. Exact accuracy was defined as perfect agreement between the predicted and benchmark triage labels, summarized both by triage level and as the unweighted mean across the four triage levels. Directional bias measures quantified relative over- and under-triage rate. To quantify directional bias, triage labels were mapped to ordinal scores (A = 1, B = 2, C = 3, D = 4), and signed ordinal error was calculated as predicted score minus benchmark score; positive values indicate over-triage, negative values indicate under-triage, and 0 indicates no net directional bias. Level-specific over- and under-triage rates were reported as the directional-rate decomposition of this bias. We additionally report directed triage error as the over-triage rate minus the under-triage rate to summarize directional asymmetry on a bounded percentage scale (from -100% to 100%). To quantify ordinal imprecision, absolute ordinal error was calculated as the absolute value of signed ordinal error, so that larger values indicate greater dispersion around the benchmark label irrespective of direction. 

### Non-directive control analysis

Our primary auditing analysis, described above, used a target model system prompt that included both the clinical context of the task (i.e., that the user query pertained to a mental health concern) and information about the set of triage dispositions (i.e., the full set of 4 triage labels and their descriptions; Table S4). Such context is likely to be missing from system prompts in consumer-facing chatbots, and arguably explicitly biases the target model towards considering mental health risks implied by user messages (akin to psychological priming or framing effects), leading to an observed over-triage pattern that is not representative of chatbot performance in production. 

Thus, we conducted a control experiment to test the robustness of our findings to less directive system prompts. In this experiment we repeated the entire auditing simulation workflow (`r nondirective_n_vignettes` vignettes, `r nondirective_n_models` target models, `r nondirective_n_items` total simulations), replacing the directive system prompt (Table S4) with a non-directive system prompt that did not contain clinical framing information. This non-directive prompt described the AI chatbot as a "general-purpose AI assistant" receiving a single message about a concern and asked it to provide a single-sentence recommendation about appropriate next steps, including the timing of any medical assessment. This altered prompt did not mention triage, urgency levels, or the A/B/C/D ontology, and it required strict JSON with only a recommendation field as output. These recommendation outputs were then post hoc classified into equivalent urgency categories (A–D, plus "medical recommendation without timing" and "other") by an independent LLM judge (Claude Sonnet 4.6, temperature 0.0, 3-replicate majority vote, Fleiss' κ = `r sprintf("%.3f", nondirective_fleiss_kappa)`; Table S4). 

### Statistical and reporting procedures

Accuracy-by-triage level differences were tested with likelihood-ratio $\chi^2$ tests from logistic regression, with correctness coded as a binary outcome at the model-vignette output level. Directional asymmetry of error (over-triage vs under-triage) and clinician relabeling direction (upward vs downward shifts) were tested with exact 2-sided binomial tests against a null probability of 0.5. Paired comparisons between original and clinician clear-only labels used continuity-corrected McNemar $\chi^2$ tests on the matched clear-only subset.

Model-level associations were summarized with Spearman rank correlations for the correspondence between model ranking under original and clinician clear-only labels, for the association between emergency under-triage and directed over-triage bias, and for the association of model release date with overall accuracy, emergency under-triage, and directed error. Ordinal bias and imprecision were summarized descriptively using signed and absolute ordinal error. A vignette-level analysis tested whether clinician-label entropy predicted model over-triage using logistic regression. All tests were 2-sided, and P \< .05 was considered statistically significant.

## Results

```{r}
#| label: results_confirmation_stats
#| include: false
fang_model_meta <- fang_df %>%
  left_join(model_meta, by = c("target_model" = "openrouter_id")) %>%
  mutate(display_model = display_model(target_model_label))

fang_macro_full <- fang_acc %>%
  left_join(distinct(fang_model_meta, target_model_label, display_model), by = "target_model_label") %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model) %>%
  summarise(macro_accuracy = mean(accuracy), .groups = "drop")

fang_clear_acc <- fang_acc %>%
  filter(!str_detect(gold_triage, "/")) %>%
  group_by(target_model_label) %>%
  summarise(macro_accuracy = mean(accuracy), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(distinct(fang_model_meta, target_model_label, display_model), by = "target_model_label")

fang_clear_df <- fang_df %>%
  left_join(model_meta, by = c("target_model" = "openrouter_id")) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = display_model(target_model_label),
    gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels),
    signed_error = purrr::map2_dbl(gold_triage, predicted_triage, directional_score_from_labels)
  ) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(as.character(gold_triage), "/"), is.finite(signed_error))

fang_full_df <- fang_df %>%
  left_join(model_meta, by = c("target_model" = "openrouter_id")) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = display_model(target_model_label),
    gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = c("A", "A/B", "B", "B/C", "C", "C/D", "D")),
    signed_error = purrr::map2_dbl(gold_triage, predicted_triage, directional_score_from_labels)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(signed_error))

matched_clear_compare <- llm_df %>%
  transmute(
    run_id,
    vignette_id,
    target_model_label,
    gold_llm = gold_triage,
    match_llm = as.integer(triage_match),
    uo_llm = under_over_triage
  ) %>%
  inner_join(
    fang_df %>%
      transmute(
        run_id,
        vignette_id,
        target_model_label,
        gold_fang = gold_triage,
        predicted_fang = predicted_triage,
        match_fang = as.integer(triage_match),
        uo_fang = under_over_triage
      ),
    by = c("run_id", "vignette_id", "target_model_label")
  ) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(gold_fang, "/"), !is.na(match_llm), !is.na(match_fang))

n_matched_clear <- nrow(matched_clear_compare)
acc_matched_llm <- mean(matched_clear_compare$match_llm)
acc_matched_fang <- mean(matched_clear_compare$match_fang)
over_matched_llm <- mean(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "over_triage")
over_matched_fang <- mean(matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "over_triage")
under_matched_llm <- mean(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "under_triage")
under_matched_fang <- mean(matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "under_triage")

acc_discordant_orig_only <- sum(matched_clear_compare$match_llm == 1 & matched_clear_compare$match_fang == 0)
acc_discordant_fang_only <- sum(matched_clear_compare$match_llm == 0 & matched_clear_compare$match_fang == 1)
acc_mcnemar <- mcnemar.test(
  matrix(
    c(
      sum(matched_clear_compare$match_llm == 1 & matched_clear_compare$match_fang == 1),
      acc_discordant_orig_only,
      acc_discordant_fang_only,
      sum(matched_clear_compare$match_llm == 0 & matched_clear_compare$match_fang == 0)
    ),
    nrow = 2
  ),
  correct = TRUE
)

over_discordant_orig_only <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "over_triage" & matched_clear_compare$uo_fang != "over_triage")
over_discordant_fang_only <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm != "over_triage" & matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "over_triage")
over_mcnemar <- mcnemar.test(
  matrix(
    c(
      sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "over_triage" & matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "over_triage"),
      over_discordant_orig_only,
      over_discordant_fang_only,
      sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm != "over_triage" & matched_clear_compare$uo_fang != "over_triage")
    ),
    nrow = 2
  ),
  correct = TRUE
)

orig_over_n <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "over_triage")
orig_under_n <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_llm == "under_triage")
fang_over_n <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "over_triage")
fang_under_n <- sum(matched_clear_compare$uo_fang == "under_triage")

orig_direction_binom <- binom.test(orig_over_n, orig_over_n + orig_under_n, p = 0.5)
fang_direction_binom <- binom.test(fang_over_n, fang_over_n + fang_under_n, p = 0.5)

orig_over_n_all <- sum(llm_scored_df$under_over_triage == "over_triage")
orig_under_n_all <- sum(llm_scored_df$under_over_triage == "under_triage")
orig_direction_binom_all <- binom.test(orig_over_n_all, orig_over_n_all + orig_under_n_all, p = 0.5)

gold_effect_fit_llm <- glm(triage_match ~ factor(gold_triage, levels = triage_levels), data = llm_scored_df, family = binomial())
gold_effect_fit_fang <- glm(match_fang ~ factor(gold_fang, levels = triage_levels), data = matched_clear_compare, family = binomial())
gold_effect_llm <- anova(gold_effect_fit_llm, test = "Chisq")
gold_effect_fang <- anova(gold_effect_fit_fang, test = "Chisq")
gold_effect_llm_chisq <- gold_effect_llm$Deviance[2]
gold_effect_llm_df <- gold_effect_llm$Df[2]
gold_effect_llm_p <- gold_effect_llm[["Pr(>Chi)"]][2]
gold_effect_fang_chisq <- gold_effect_fang$Deviance[2]
gold_effect_fang_df <- gold_effect_fang$Df[2]
gold_effect_fang_p <- gold_effect_fang[["Pr(>Chi)"]][2]

clear_label_shift <- llm_df %>%
  distinct(vignette_id, original_gold = gold_triage) %>%
  inner_join(
    fang_df %>%
      distinct(vignette_id, clinician_clear_gold = gold_triage) %>%
      filter(!str_detect(clinician_clear_gold, "/")),
    by = "vignette_id"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    original_gold = factor(original_gold, levels = triage_levels),
    clinician_clear_gold = factor(clinician_clear_gold, levels = triage_levels),
    shift = triage_numeric(clinician_clear_gold) - triage_numeric(original_gold)
  )

n_clear_shift_up <- sum(clear_label_shift$shift > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_clear_shift_same <- sum(clear_label_shift$shift == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_clear_shift_down <- sum(clear_label_shift$shift < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_clear_overlap_total <- nrow(clear_label_shift)
p_clear_shift_same <- n_clear_shift_same / n_clear_overlap_total

clear_shift_direction_binom <- binom.test(n_clear_shift_up, n_clear_shift_up + n_clear_shift_down, p = 0.5)

clear_shift_counts <- clear_label_shift %>%
  count(original_gold, clinician_clear_gold, name = "n")

n_shift_B_to_C <- clear_shift_counts %>%
  filter(original_gold == "B", clinician_clear_gold == "C") %>%
  pull(n) %>%
  sum(na.rm = TRUE)

n_shift_C_to_D <- clear_shift_counts %>%
  filter(original_gold == "C", clinician_clear_gold == "D") %>%
  pull(n) %>%
  sum(na.rm = TRUE)

fang_emergency_clear <- matched_clear_compare %>%
  filter(gold_fang == "D")

n_fang_emergency_cases <- nrow(fang_emergency_clear)
n_fang_emergency_undertriage <- sum(fang_emergency_clear$uo_fang == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE)
p_fang_emergency_undertriage <- n_fang_emergency_undertriage / n_fang_emergency_cases

fang_emergency_under_predictions <- fang_emergency_clear %>%
  filter(uo_fang == "under_triage") %>%
  count(predicted_fang, sort = TRUE)

all_fang_emergency_undertriage_to_C <- nrow(fang_emergency_under_predictions) == 1 &&
  fang_emergency_under_predictions$predicted_fang[1] == "C"

fang_D_matched_compare <- matched_clear_compare %>%
  filter(gold_fang == "D")

emergency_discordant_orig_only <- sum(fang_D_matched_compare$uo_llm == "under_triage" & fang_D_matched_compare$uo_fang != "under_triage")
emergency_discordant_fang_only <- sum(fang_D_matched_compare$uo_llm != "under_triage" & fang_D_matched_compare$uo_fang == "under_triage")
emergency_under_mcnemar <- mcnemar.test(
  matrix(
    c(
      sum(fang_D_matched_compare$uo_llm == "under_triage" & fang_D_matched_compare$uo_fang == "under_triage"),
      emergency_discordant_orig_only,
      emergency_discordant_fang_only,
      sum(fang_D_matched_compare$uo_llm != "under_triage" & fang_D_matched_compare$uo_fang != "under_triage")
    ),
    nrow = 2
  ),
  correct = TRUE
)

fang_level_accuracy <- matched_clear_compare %>%
  group_by(gold_fang) %>%
  summarise(mean_accuracy = mean(match_fang), .groups = "drop") %>%
  transmute(gold_triage = gold_fang, mean_accuracy)

acc_fang_A <- get_level_metric("A", mean_accuracy, fang_level_accuracy)
acc_fang_B <- get_level_metric("B", mean_accuracy, fang_level_accuracy)
acc_fang_C <- get_level_metric("C", mean_accuracy, fang_level_accuracy)
acc_fang_D <- get_level_metric("D", mean_accuracy, fang_level_accuracy)

llm_level_direction_rates <- llm_scored_df %>%
  group_by(gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(
    over_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "over_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    under_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

orig_over_A <- get_level_metric("A", over_rate, llm_level_direction_rates)
orig_over_B <- get_level_metric("B", over_rate, llm_level_direction_rates)
orig_over_C <- get_level_metric("C", over_rate, llm_level_direction_rates)
orig_over_D <- get_level_metric("D", over_rate, llm_level_direction_rates)

fang_level_direction_rates <- matched_clear_compare %>%
  group_by(gold_fang) %>%
  summarise(
    over_rate = mean(uo_fang == "over_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    under_rate = mean(uo_fang == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  transmute(gold_triage = gold_fang, over_rate, under_rate)

fang_over_A <- get_level_metric("A", over_rate, fang_level_direction_rates)
fang_over_B <- get_level_metric("B", over_rate, fang_level_direction_rates)
fang_over_C <- get_level_metric("C", over_rate, fang_level_direction_rates)
fang_over_D <- get_level_metric("D", over_rate, fang_level_direction_rates)

model_accuracy_alignment <- macro_by_model %>%
  select(target_model_label, macro_accuracy_llm = macro_accuracy) %>%
  inner_join(
    fang_clear_acc %>%
      select(target_model_label, macro_accuracy_fang = macro_accuracy),
    by = "target_model_label"
  )

overall_accuracy_rank_corr <- suppressWarnings(
  cor.test(
    model_accuracy_alignment$macro_accuracy_llm,
    model_accuracy_alignment$macro_accuracy_fang,
    method = "spearman",
    exact = FALSE
  )
)

matched_both_D <- matched_clear_compare %>%
  filter(gold_llm == "D", gold_fang == "D")

n_both_D_vignettes <- n_distinct(matched_both_D$vignette_id)
n_both_D_cases <- nrow(matched_both_D)
n_both_D_undertriage <- sum(matched_both_D$uo_llm == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE)
p_both_D_undertriage <- n_both_D_undertriage / n_both_D_cases

both_D_under_predictions <- matched_both_D %>%
  filter(uo_llm == "under_triage") %>%
  count(predicted_fang, sort = TRUE)

all_both_D_undertriage_to_C <- nrow(both_D_under_predictions) == 1 &&
  both_D_under_predictions$predicted_fang[1] == "C"
```

```{r}
#| label: results_model_rankings
#| include: false
results_table1 <- acc_llm %>%
  select(target_model_label, display_model, provider, gold_triage, accuracy) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = gold_triage, values_from = accuracy) %>%
  left_join(macro_by_model, by = c("target_model_label", "display_model", "provider")) %>%
  left_join(model_ordinal_error, by = c("target_model_label", "display_model", "provider")) %>%
  left_join(runs_by_model, by = c("target_model_label", "display_model")) %>%
  arrange(desc(D), desc(macro_accuracy), display_model) %>%
  transmute(
    Model = display_model,
    Provider = provider,
    `N completed` = n_completed_model,
    A = fmt_pct(A),
    B = fmt_pct(B),
    C = fmt_pct(C),
    D = fmt_pct(D),
    `Overall accuracy` = fmt_pct(macro_accuracy),
    `Mean signed ordinal error` = fmt_signed_num(mean_signed_ordinal_error),
    `Mean absolute ordinal error` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_absolute_ordinal_error)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: results_emergency_undertriage
#| include: false
collapse_with_and <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  if (length(x) == 0) {
    return("")
  }
  if (length(x) == 1) {
    return(x)
  }
  if (length(x) == 2) {
    return(paste(x, collapse = " and "))
  }
  paste0(paste(x[-length(x)], collapse = ", "), ", and ", x[length(x)])
}

llm_emergency_df <- llm_scored_df %>%
  filter(as.character(gold_triage) == "D")

n_emergency_cases <- nrow(llm_emergency_df)
n_emergency_undertriage <- sum(llm_emergency_df$under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE)
p_emergency_undertriage <- n_emergency_undertriage / n_emergency_cases

emergency_under_predictions <- llm_emergency_df %>%
  filter(under_over_triage == "under_triage") %>%
  count(predicted_triage, sort = TRUE)

all_emergency_undertriage_to_C <- nrow(emergency_under_predictions) == 1 &&
  emergency_under_predictions$predicted_triage[1] == "C"

emergency_under_by_model <- llm_emergency_df %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model) %>%
  summarise(
    n_emergency = n(),
    n_under = sum(under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    under_rate = n_under / n_emergency,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(display_model)

zero_emergency_under_models <- emergency_under_by_model %>%
  filter(n_under == 0) %>%
  pull(display_model)

zero_emergency_under_models_text <- collapse_with_and(zero_emergency_under_models)

top5_models <- macro_by_model %>% slice_head(n = 5)
n_top5_with_emergency_undertriage <- emergency_under_by_model %>%
  semi_join(top5_models, by = c("target_model_label", "display_model")) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(n_under > 0)) %>%
  pull(n)

model_error_bias <- llm_scored_df %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model) %>%
  summarise(
    over_rate = mean(under_over_triage == "over_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    under_rate_all = mean(under_over_triage == "under_triage", na.rm = TRUE),
    directed_error = over_rate - under_rate_all,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

emergency_bias_compare <- emergency_under_by_model %>%
  left_join(model_error_bias, by = c("target_model_label", "display_model"))

zero_emergency_under_mean_directed <- emergency_bias_compare %>%
  filter(n_under == 0) %>%
  summarise(mean_directed = mean(directed_error)) %>%
  pull(mean_directed)

nonzero_emergency_under_mean_directed <- emergency_bias_compare %>%
  filter(n_under > 0) %>%
  summarise(mean_directed = mean(directed_error)) %>%
  pull(mean_directed)

emergency_under_over_corr <- suppressWarnings(
  cor.test(
    emergency_bias_compare$under_rate,
    emergency_bias_compare$directed_error,
    method = "spearman",
    exact = FALSE
  )
)

release_date_compare <- macro_by_model %>%
  select(target_model_label, display_model, overall_accuracy = macro_accuracy) %>%
  left_join(
    emergency_under_by_model %>%
      select(target_model_label, emergency_under_rate = under_rate),
    by = "target_model_label"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    model_error_bias %>%
      select(target_model_label, directed_error),
    by = "target_model_label"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    llm_df %>%
      distinct(target_model_label, release_date),
    by = "target_model_label"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(release_date))

release_corr_overall_accuracy <- suppressWarnings(
  cor.test(
    as.numeric(release_date_compare$release_date),
    release_date_compare$overall_accuracy,
    method = "spearman",
    exact = FALSE
  )
)

release_corr_emergency_under <- suppressWarnings(
  cor.test(
    as.numeric(release_date_compare$release_date),
    release_date_compare$emergency_under_rate,
    method = "spearman",
    exact = FALSE
  )
)

release_corr_directed_error <- suppressWarnings(
  cor.test(
    as.numeric(release_date_compare$release_date),
    release_date_compare$directed_error,
    method = "spearman",
    exact = FALSE
  )
)
```

### Emergency under-triage

Emergency under-triage was defined as any target AI chatbot assignment below level D (i.e., predicted triage A, B, or C) for a level D vignette. Across the `r n_models` evaluated target AI chatbots, emergency under-triage relative to the original benchmark labels was rare, occurring in only `r n_emergency_undertriage` of `r n_emergency_cases` trials (`r fmt_pct(p_emergency_undertriage)` of level D trials; Figure 2A,C). All undertriaged emergencies were reassigned to level C (assessment within 24 to 48 hours), and emergency under-triage rates were 0% in `r zero_emergency_under_models_text`.



When considering the set of vignettes assigned a clinician consensus label of D, emergency under-triage was similarly low (`r fmt_pct(p_fang_emergency_undertriage)`, `r n_fang_emergency_undertriage` of `r n_fang_emergency_cases` trials), with all under-triage trials being assigned to level C (urgent care). Restricting further to vignettes labeled D by both the original benchmark labels and the clinician clear-consensus labels (`r n_both_D_vignettes` vignettes), emergency under-triage remained low across all target models (`r n_both_D_undertriage` of `r n_both_D_cases` trials, `r fmt_pct(p_both_D_undertriage)`), again always as reassignment to level C.

### Overall accuracy and level-wise accuracy

Overall accuracy was defined as the proportion of predicted triage labels that matched the original benchmark labels, summarized as the unweighted mean accuracy across levels A to D. Across the `r n_models` evaluated frontier AI chatbots, overall accuracy relative to the original benchmark labels ranged from `r fmt_pct(min(macro_by_model$macro_accuracy))` to `r fmt_pct(max(macro_by_model$macro_accuracy))` (Table S3; Figure 2). `r best_model` had the highest overall accuracy (`r fmt_pct(best_model_accuracy)`), followed by `r display_model(top3_models$target_model_label[2])` (`r fmt_pct(top3_models$macro_accuracy[2])`) and `r display_model(top3_models$target_model_label[3])` (`r fmt_pct(top3_models$macro_accuracy[3])`). Importantly, however, the 5 highest-accuracy AI chatbots each showed non-zero emergency under-triage rates, pointing to a potential trade-off between higher overall accuracy at lower-acuity presentations, at the expense of under-triage for more serious presentations.

Accuracy differed significantly across the original benchmark triage levels ($\chi^2_{`r gold_effect_llm_df`}$ = `r round(gold_effect_llm_chisq, 1)`, P `r fmt_p(gold_effect_llm_p)`; logistic regression). Level D (emergency) vignettes showed the highest AI chatbot-averaged accuracy (`r fmt_pct(acc_D)`), whereas level B (intermediate) vignettes showed the lowest (`r fmt_pct(acc_B)`). Levels A (routine) and C (urgent) showed intermediate accuracies of `r fmt_pct(acc_A)` and `r fmt_pct(acc_C)`, respectively, yielding a U-shaped calibration profile in which the intermediate-acuity levels were hardest to distinguish. AI chatbots may therefore be better at recognizing the extremes of psychiatric urgency than at estimating the timing threshold that separates intermediate from urgent assessment.

Repeating this analysis using clinician-rated consensus triage labels yielded a qualitatively similar pattern of results (correlation model-level overall accuracies, Spearman $\rho$ = `r round(unname(overall_accuracy_rank_corr$estimate), 2)`, P `r fmt_p(overall_accuracy_rank_corr$p.value)`). Overall accuracy was higher under clinician clear-consensus labels than under the original benchmark labels (`r fmt_pct(acc_matched_fang)` vs `r fmt_pct(acc_matched_llm)`; McNemar $\chi^2_1$ = `r round(unname(acc_mcnemar$statistic), 1)`, P `r fmt_p(acc_mcnemar$p.value)`). Accuracy differed significantly across clinician clear-consensus triage levels ($\chi^2_{`r gold_effect_fang_df`}$ = `r round(gold_effect_fang_chisq, 1)`, P `r fmt_p(gold_effect_fang_p)`; logistic regression), with level D vignettes showing the highest accuracy (`r fmt_pct(acc_fang_D)`) and level B vignettes the lowest (`r fmt_pct(acc_fang_B)`); levels A and C again showed intermediate accuracies (`r fmt_pct(acc_fang_A)` and `r fmt_pct(acc_fang_C)`, respectively).

```{r}
#| label: figure2_data
#| include: false
model_axis_order <- llm_df %>%
  distinct(target_model_label, display_model, provider, family, release_date) %>%
  mutate(
    model_family = case_when(
      str_detect(display_model, "^Claude") ~ "Claude",
      str_detect(display_model, "^Gemini") ~ "Gemini",
      str_detect(display_model, "^GPT") ~ "GPT",
      str_detect(display_model, "^Grok") ~ "Grok",
      str_detect(display_model, "^DeepSeek") ~ "DeepSeek",
      str_detect(display_model, "^Llama") ~ "Llama",
      str_detect(display_model, "^Mistral") ~ "Mistral",
      str_detect(display_model, "^Qwen") ~ "Qwen",
      TRUE ~ coalesce(family, as.character(provider), display_model)
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(model_family, release_date, display_model)

provider_model_order <- model_axis_order$display_model

model_fill_values <- setNames(model_palette[provider_model_order], provider_model_order)

model_family_boundaries <- model_axis_order %>%
  mutate(model_index = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(model_family) %>%
  summarise(last_index = max(model_index), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(boundary = last_index + 0.5) %>%
  filter(boundary < length(provider_model_order) + 0.5)

overall_accuracy_ci_df <- llm_scored_df %>%
  mutate(
    provider = factor(provider, levels = names(provider_palette)),
    correct = as.integer(triage_match)
  ) %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci_95(correct), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(measure = factor("Overall", levels = measure_levels))

level_accuracy_ci_df <- llm_scored_df %>%
  mutate(
    provider = factor(provider, levels = names(provider_palette)),
    gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels),
    correct = as.integer(triage_match)
  ) %>%
  group_by(target_model_label, display_model, provider, gold_triage) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci_95(correct), .groups = "drop") %>%
  transmute(
    target_model_label,
    display_model,
    provider,
    n,
    mean,
    lower,
    upper,
    measure = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = measure_levels)
  )

panel_a_df <- bind_rows(
  overall_accuracy_ci_df %>% select(target_model_label, display_model, provider, n, mean, lower, upper, measure),
  level_accuracy_ci_df
) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = factor(display_model, levels = provider_model_order),
    measure = factor(measure, levels = measure_levels)
  )

panel_a_bar_width <- 0.14
panel_a_offsets <- c("Overall" = -0.32, "A" = -0.16, "B" = 0.00, "C" = 0.16, "D" = 0.32)

panel_a_df <- panel_a_df %>%
  mutate(
    model_index = match(as.character(display_model), provider_model_order),
    x_center = model_index + unname(panel_a_offsets[as.character(measure)])
  )

panel_a_overall_df <- panel_a_df %>%
  filter(measure == "Overall")

panel_a_labels <- panel_a_overall_df %>%
  mutate(
    label = fmt_pct(mean, digits = 1),
    x_label = model_index
  )

panel_a_level_df <- panel_a_df %>%
  filter(measure != "Overall")

panel_rate_bar_width <- 0.16
under_panel_levels <- c("B", "C", "D")
over_panel_levels <- c("A", "B", "C")
under_panel_offsets <- c("B" = -0.16, "C" = 0.00, "D" = 0.16)
over_panel_offsets <- c("A" = -0.16, "B" = 0.00, "C" = 0.16)
panel_rate_overall_offset <- -0.40
top_panel_y_limits <- c(-0.03, 1.06)

main_confusion_panel <- plot_confusion_heatmap(
  confusion_long(llm_scored_df, normalize_rows = TRUE),
  title = "A",
  subtitle = "All models pooled",
  normalize_rows = TRUE
)

main_accuracy_plot <- ggplot(
  panel_a_level_df,
  aes(x = x_center, y = mean, fill = display_model, alpha = measure)
) +
  geom_col(
    width = panel_a_bar_width,
    color = NA,
    linewidth = 0
  ) +
  geom_linerange(
    data = panel_a_overall_df,
    aes(x = x_center, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, color = display_model),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = panel_a_overall_df,
    aes(x = x_center, y = mean, color = display_model),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = manuscript_point_size + 0.15,
    shape = 16
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = panel_a_level_df,
    aes(x = x_center, y = 0.028, label = as.character(measure)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = base_font_size * 0.20,
    vjust = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = panel_a_labels,
    aes(x = x_label, y = 1.02, label = label, color = display_model),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = base_font_size * 0.28,
    vjust = 0,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = model_family_boundaries,
    aes(xintercept = boundary),
    color = "grey82",
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_fill_values, guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_fill_values, guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = measure_alpha_values, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq_along(provider_model_order),
    labels = provider_model_order,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.03, 0.03))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1), limits = top_panel_y_limits, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Accuracy",
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "AI chatbot overall and level-specific accuracy relative to original benchmark labels"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```
